patch.value: pm update doesn't accept --expected/--actual aliases that pm close accepts

Inconsistency observed 2026-05-28: PR #78 added --expected/--actual inline aliases for pm close (mapping to expected_result/actual_result). However pm update STILL requires --expected-result/--actual-result — passing --expected to pm update fails with 'missing: --expected-result'. Either pm update should accept the same short aliases for consistency, or pm close should drop them. Likely fix: extend the alias mapping in src/cli/register-mutation.ts (or wherever update's commander spec lives) to register --expected and --actual as aliases of --expected-result/--actual-result for update as well. This is what agents naturally type after pm close added them.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Related UX inconsistency observed in same session: 'pm update pm-XXX --add-tags fix' rejects with 'missing: --tags'. The retry suggestion is even worse — it says 'pm update pm-ydkl --tags fix' which REPLACES all tags (losing existing ones) instead of adding. Either (a) pm update should accept --add-tags as it does in other places, or (b) the retry hint should preserve existing tags. Same root cause as the --expected vs --expected-result inconsistency: flag aliases that exist on one mutation but not another.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Branch feat/agent-ux-tag-mutations-and-result-aliases — implementing --add-tags/--remove-tags on update+create+update-many and --expected/--actual short aliases on update+close (+ update-many). Resolves the two reported inconsistencies. Will also remove the now-obsolete --add-tags->--tags semantic suggestion since the additive flag is now native.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Implementing additive tag mutations + expected/actual alias parity

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Branch feat/agent-ux-tag-mutations-and-result-aliases implements both halves of this issue: (1) --expected/--actual short aliases now accepted on pm update + pm create + pm update-many (matching pm close), wired through commander-mutation-options contracts + register-mutation + CLOSE/CREATE/UPDATE/UPDATE_MANY flag contracts in cli-contracts.ts. (2) Additive tag mutations: --add-tags (create/update/update-many) and --remove-tags (update/update-many) via new core helpers mergeAdditiveTags/applyTagRemovals/collectTagFlagValues in core/item/parse.ts (CSV + JSON-array entries, sorted+deduped+lowercased). NEW BUG FOUND & FIXED during dogfooding: update-many --add-tags/--remove-tags reported updated_count:0 skipped_count:N because buildPlannedItemDiff ignored additive tag ops -> empty diff -> every row skipped. Added buildTagMutationPlan() replicating runUpdate's tags resolution order (replace->add->remove) so dry-run preview AND apply-mode actionable detection both see additive tag changes. Removed now-dead tags scalar branch + splitCommaList import. Verified in temp dir: create/update/update-many/close all correct. 1970 unit tests green.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Sub-agent work (orchestrator-recorded to avoid concurrent history writes): (A) docs specialist updated docs/COMMANDS.md (+17: --tags REPLACES vs --add-tags/--remove-tags additive note + examples + --expected/--actual alias para), docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md (+7: canonical-loop accumulation note), docs/QUICKSTART.md (+3). Noted gap: close inline result fields (--resolution/--expected-result/--actual-result) still undocumented in these files (pre-existing; candidate follow-up). (B) completion specialist updated src/cli/commands/completion.ts zsh+fish for create/update/update-many (--add-tags, --remove-tags, --expected, --actual). Orchestrator additionally completed the close completion section which was missing ALL inline closure flags (--reason/--close-reason/--resolution/--expected-result/--actual-result + new --expected/--actual) in both zsh and fish — now matches CLOSE_FLAG_CONTRACTS. tsc clean.

patch.value: Added --expected/--actual short aliases to pm update + create + update-many (matching pm close), and additive tag mutations --add-tags (create/update/update-many) + --remove-tags (update/update-many). Fixed update-many actionable-detection bug where additive-tag-only updates were skipped. Wired through commander contracts, cli-contracts flag contracts, MCP (camelCase passthrough), docs, and zsh/fish completions.
pm update/create/update-many accept --expected/--actual and --add-tags/--remove-tags consistently with pm close; additive tag ops never silently skipped
Verified: all flags accepted and applied correctly in temp-dir dogfooding; 1973 tests + 100% coverage green
Implemented additive tag mutations and expected/actual alias parity across all mutation commands

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #82 opened (feat/agent-ux-tag-mutations-and-result-aliases -> main): https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/pull/82 . Companion pm-changelog PR #20 (pmc-874d). Bot reviews triggered (codex/gemini/gemini-code-assist/coderabbit/sourcery). Waiting 20 min for CI + bot feedback.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Observability verified post-change: (1) Sentry (unbrained/pm-cli) returns data — 4 pre-existing issues (PM-CLI-Z calendar positional + Y/V/W temp-dir test-harness noise), ZERO new unresolved errors in last 24h from this work. (2) Telemetry stack healthy: all 10 containers up 7d, API ok v2026.5.28, 12441 Postgres events/24h, RabbitMQ queue draining (0 backlog), latest command_start/finish timestamps real-time (this session's pm create/update/close/update-many captured live). End-to-end telemetry confirmed working.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #82 review round 1 addressed (commit 42e51d6e). Codex P2 #1: dropped list:true from add-tags/remove-tags contracts (bootstrap coalescer was corrupting JSON-array form of repeated occurrences). Codex P2 #2: added addTags/removeTags to tool schema (TOOL_*_OPTION_CONTRACTS + PM_TOOL_PARAMETER_PROPERTIES) for additionalProperties:false MCP/SDK clients. Gemini: lowercased baseTags in mergeAdditiveTags/applyTagRemovals (case-insensitive dedup+removal); pm update skips no-op tag writes. Declined gemini's normalizeExistingTags-lowercase in update-many (would skip legitimate case-normalization writes — RAW comparison is correct). Companion pm-changelog PR #20 round 1 also addressed (commit ee22d5d): strong(type/tag) vs weak(title) Changed-signal split so explicit refactor tag wins over Issue->Fixed default; typeof guard on item.type. Both: 100% coverage/all gates green. Re-triggered codex+gemini.
